sections.content: Recursion is a programming technique in which a function calls itself to solve a problem.
Recursion is the process of solving a problem by breaking it into smaller subproblems of the same type.
A recursive function is a function that directly or indirectly calls itself.
The base condition is the stopping condition that prevents infinite recursive calls.

content: If condition is false, function calls itself.
If condition is true, recursion stops.
Useful for tree and graph traversal

sections.content: A function calls another function which eventually calls the original function.
Factorial of n is calculated as n × factorial(n-1) until n becomes 1.
Each recursive call creates a new stack frame in memory.

summary: Recursion is a function calling itself.
A base condition is necessary to stop recursion.
Recursion is useful for solving complex problems.
Each recursive call creates a stack frame.
Factorial and Fibonacci are common recursion examples.
